role: Iranian intelligence operative; convicted perpetrator of the Mykonos assassination
bio: Kazem Darabi is an Iranian national convicted by a German court for his role as the principal organiser of the [Mykonos restaurant assassination](/events/mykonos-restaurant-assassination-1992) in Berlin on 17 September 1992. Working as a greengrocer and Iranian intelligence asset in Germany, Darabi coordinated the attack in which PDKI Secretary-General [Sadegh Sharafkandi](/people/sadegh-sharafkandi) and three associates were shot dead. Darabi recruited [Abbas Rhayel](/people/abbas-rhayel), an experienced [Hezbollah](/orgs/hezbollah) operative, as one of the two gunmen who carried out the killings. He was convicted by the Berlin Court of Appeals in April 1997 and sentenced to life imprisonment. The court found that the killings were ordered by Iran's Committee for Special Operations — a body that included [Ali Khamenei](/people/ali-khamenei), [Akbar Hashemi Rafsanjani](/people/akbar-hashemi-rafsanjani), [Ali Fallahian](/people/ali-fallahian), and [Ali Akbar Velayati](/people/ali-akbar-velayati). Darabi was released and deported to Iran in December 2007 after serving ten years.
editorial_notes: Convicted April 1997. Sentenced to life, released and deported to Iran December 2007. Operated as MOIS asset in Germany under cover as greengrocer.
